pr.title: Begin transition of sysdolphin asm into matching C
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Started migrating sysdolphin assembly into matching C by adding initial C implementations and struct headers for AObj, WObj, displayfunc, and initialize paths. The PR was closed unmerged, but it captured an early transition pattern: keep remaining assembly in renamed *_asm.s files, add C objects beside them in the linker script, and progressively replace anonymous func_ symbols with HSD_* names. The author noted several functions still needed non-matching ifdefs because of epilogue/codegen issues.
what_changed: Added initial C implementations for AObj routines including HSD_AObjInitAllocData, HSD_AObjGetAllocData, HSD_AObjGetFlags, HSD_AObjSetFlags, HSD_AObjClearFlags, HSD_AObjSetFObj, HSD_AObjInitEndCallBack, HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks, and HSD_AObjReqAnim.
Added WObj C routines HSD_WObjRemoveAnim and HSD_WObjReqAnim, calling through to AObj and RObj animation helpers.
Added initialize.c with HSD_InitComponent, HSD_GXSetFifoObj, and an empty HSD_DVDInit stub, wiring together OS, VI, GX, DVD, ID, object, and retrace initialization calls.
Added displayfunc.c with HSD_ZListInitAllocData and a local HSD_ZList layout used for object allocation sizing.
Introduced sysdolphin headers for AObj, FObj, WObj, VI status, displayfunc, initialize, and singly linked lists, providing struct layouts and prototypes needed for C decompilation.
Added include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h with GX types and constants such as GXFifoObj, GXRenderModeObj, GXColor, GX_TRUE/GX_FALSE, GX_ENABLE/GX_DISABLE, GX_GM_1_0, and GX_MAX_Z24-related dependencies.

Renamed assembly files to keep residual assembly separate from new C objects: asm/aobj.s to asm/aobj_asm.s, asm/initialize.s to asm/initialize_asm.s, asm/displayfunc.s to asm/displayfunc_asm.s, and wobj assembly to asm/wobj_asm.s.
Updated ldscript.lcf to link both the new C objects and the remaining *_asm.o objects in sysdolphin order.

Updated include/functions.h prototypes and many assembly callsites to replace anonymous func_ names with HSD_* names such as HSD_AObjRemove, HSD_AObjReqAnim, HSD_FObjRemoveAll, HSD_FObjReqAnimAll, HSD_WObjReqAnim, HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAll, and HSD_RObjReqAnimAll.
Changed Makefile CFLAGS by removing -proc gekko while retaining -Cpp_exceptions off, -fp hard, and -O4,p.
Included GX and MTX headers from include/global.h so new sysdolphin structs could reference GXRenderModeObj, GXColor, GXFifoObj, Mtx, Vec, and related types.
Normalized missing trailing newlines in several existing sysdolphin/source headers and files.
smart_moves: Kept mixed C/ASM migration linkable by moving only decompiled functions into C while preserving undecompiled code in renamed *_asm.s files and explicitly ordering both in ldscript.lcf.
Used real HSD_* symbol names across unrelated assembly callsites once functions were identified, improving future grepability and making cross-system animation ownership clearer.
Added struct headers before or alongside C functions, allowing function prototypes to use typed pointers instead of opaque or guessed signatures.
Used HSD_ObjAllocInit with sizeof(struct) in C replacements, which documents allocator object sizes for HSD_AObj and HSD_ZList instead of leaving them as raw immediate values in assembly.

Documented matching hacks directly in code: force_active was used for HSD_AObjGetAllocData, altivec pragmas were used for epilogue ordering, and optimization_level 2 was used to address a redundant branch issue.

Preserved evidence of unknown globals with address-label externs plus comments naming likely roles, such as aobj_alloc_data, FrameBuffer, memReport, iparam_fifo_size, DefaultFifoObj, init_done, and rmode.
decomp_lessons: When introducing C into a still-assembly sysdolphin file, split the original assembly into a *_asm.s remainder and add both C and asm objects to the linker script in the original text order.

Progressive symbol naming has large downstream value: renaming func_8036410C to HSD_AObjReqAnim and func_803644CC to HSD_AObjRemove enabled many callsites in cobj, fog, jobj, pobj, lobj, mobj, and gameplay assembly to become self-describing without decompiling those files.
Allocator init functions are good early decomp targets because they often reduce to HSD_ObjAllocInit(data, sizeof(type), align), simultaneously validating struct size.
Small accessor/mutator functions such as flag getters/setters are useful for proving bit constants and field offsets in new structs.
Matching may depend on CodeWarrior pragma choices even for logically simple functions; this PR explicitly tied some mismatches to epilogue addi/mtlr ordering and redundant branching.
Use typed forward declarations in include/functions.h once structures exist, but keep names conservative when only label-based evidence exists.
Initialization functions often require broad type groundwork first: HSD_InitComponent needed GXRenderModeObj, GXColor, GXFifoObj, HSD_VIStatus, and prototypes for OS/VI/GX/DVD/object/id setup calls.
naming_conventions: HSD sysdolphin public-style functions use HSD_<Object><Verb> naming, for example HSD_AObjReqAnim, HSD_AObjRemove, HSD_WObjReqAnim, HSD_FObjRemoveAll, and HSD_RObjReqAnimAll.
Object structs use typedef struct _HSD_XObj { ... } HSD_XObj; style, shown for HSD_AObj, HSD_AObjDesc, HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_FObj, HSD_FObjDesc, HSD_WObj, HSD_WObjDesc, HSD_WObjInfo, and HSD_WObjAnim.
Animation flag macros in aobj.h use AOBJ_* names with bit shifts, such as AOBJ_REWINDED, AOBJ_FIRST_PLAY, AOBJ_NO_UPDATE, AOBJ_LOOP, and AOBJ_NO_ANIM.
GX constants and typedefs follow Dolphin SDK-style names such as GXRenderModeObj, GXColor, GX_TRUE, GX_ENABLE, GX_GM_1_0, and GX_FIFO_OBJSIZE.
Residual assembly files were named with a *_asm.s suffix once their matching C counterpart existed, for example aobj_asm.s, initialize_asm.s, displayfunc_asm.s, and wobj_asm.s.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved matched assembly ranges out of asm/aobj.s and into src/sysdolphin/aobj.c, leaving later AObj code in asm/aobj_asm.s.
Added linker entries for aobj_asm.o, displayfunc_asm.o, initialize_asm.o, and wobj_asm.o immediately near their matching C object counterparts to maintain section order.
Used #pragma force_active on for HSD_AObjGetAllocData because the function was unused until a future GetAllocData function table was loaded.
Used #pragma altivec_codegen on and #pragma altivec_model on around HSD_AObjSetFObj and HSD_WObjReqAnim to affect epilogue addi/mtlr ordering.
Used #pragma optimization_level 2 around HSD_AObjInvokeCallBacks with an in-code note that it fixed a redundant branching issue.

Replaced raw branch targets in assembly with named functions where identities became known, including HSD_FObjReqAnimAll, HSD_FObjRemoveAll, HSD_AObjReqAnim, HSD_AObjRemove, HSD_WObjReqAnim, HSD_RObjRemoveAnimAll, and HSD_RObjReqAnimAll.

The PR body says several functions still had non-matching ifdefs due to epilogues; exact ifdef locations are not visible in the provided excerpt, but the added pragmas corroborate codegen/epilogue matching workarounds.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so there is no visible maintainer approval or merge rationale in this slice.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs that merged AObj/WObj/initialize C to see which pragmas or non-matching ifdefs were removed after compiler/version fixes.
Search for later replacements of lbl_804C0880, lbl_804D7628, lbl_804D762C, lbl_804D7630, lbl_804C0948, lbl_804C0954, and lbl_804D76C4 with named globals.
Verify final struct layouts for HSD_AObj, HSD_FObj, HSD_WObj, HSD_VIStatus, and HSD_ZList against later accepted headers.
Investigate whether removing -proc gekko from CFLAGS persisted in later project history or was reverted.
Compare later include/dolphin/gx/gxtypes.h guard naming because this slice shows #ifndef _gxtypes_h_ paired with #define _gxtype_h_.
